description.value:
Once per turn, when you would hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cast this jutsu to gain a +1d4 bonus to the damage roll. You can then make an additional weapon attack as part of the same action that does not add your ability modifier to its damage. You can choose to apply one of the following benefits to additional attacks made in this way and can select a different benefit for each attack made. You can only use each of this jutsu's benefits twice per casting:

Striking Assault:

  • On a successful hit, the target must make a Constitution saving throw, becoming Bruised on a failure. If this attack critically hits, the saving throw is made at disadvantage.

Slashing Assault:

  • The attack cannot be made at disadvantage, and on a critical hit, the target's AC is reduced by 1 until the end of your next turn.

Severing Assault:

  • On a successful hit, the target must make a Strength saving throw. On a failure, they become Slowed. If this attack critically hits, the saving throw is made at disadvantage.

Twice per casting, this jutsu gains additional effects based on whether Virtue or Cruelty is active:

Virtue:

  • Attacks made as a result of this jutsu treat the creature's AC as being -1 lower for each unique condition you have applied to them, to a maximum of -5.

Cruelty:

  • If an attack made by this jutsu would exceed the target's AC by 5 or more, that attack is treated as a critical hit.

At Higher Ranks:

For each rank you cast this jutsu above D-Rank, increase the cost by 3 and the number of additional attacks you can make per casting by +1.

If this jutsu is cast at B-Rank or higher, additional attacks made can apply half of your ability modifier to their damage.

If this jutsu is cast at S-Rank, additional attacks made can instead add your full ability modifier to their damage.
